title: Clank & Slop — The Tape
tagline: — READ THE TAPE · ALL THE SLOP THAT'S FIT TO PRICE —

props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: Crude is back where it started. Brent near $71.72 and WTI near $68.47 put oil around pre-war levels, and the Brent curve flipped into contango for the first time this year, the clearest sign the market has stopped pricing an immediate Hormuz shortage. Passage through the strait has widened and flows are rebuilding, though the Doha channel is only paused.

The yen got a reprieve, not a rescue. A soft US June payrolls print cut near-term Fed-hike odds and pulled USD/JPY back toward 161 from its 162.66 four-decade low. Tokyo's ledger still shows zero disclosed intervention, and the US-Japan rate gap that drove the slide has not closed.

China's open-weight models keep undercutting the closed-model economics that US chip valuations lean on. The Philadelphia semiconductor index fell 5.5% on 2 July as investors reweighed AI-hardware demand, even as the broad market held near records.

props.caption: Ukraine's strikes on Russian energy turn into fuel rationing on the Black Sea coast

items.why: The desk's open call sits at p=0.45 that USD/JPY prints a new four-decade low before month-end; watch the MOF disclosure index and US yields for whether the payroll reprieve holds or the rate gap wins.

items.what: Doha · does the next round convene
items.why: Mediators paused the US-Iran channel until after the 9 July funeral of Iran's Supreme Leader; a scheduled round would test whether the oil calm rests on more than exhaustion.
Fuel rationing on Russia's Black Sea coast and mass strikes on Ukrainian cities are now moving together; watch whether either side's pressure forces a change at the table.
Washington declined to renew the pact in its current form; the next meeting sets whether revision or drift follows for North American supply chains.
The 5.5% chip selloff lands as cheap Chinese open weights spread; watch whether it reads as a valuation reset or an early demand warning for accelerators.

open_calls.question: USD/JPY prints above 162.66 (a fresh 40-year yen low)
open_calls.detail: June's soft US payrolls cut near-term Fed-hike odds and gave the yen a reprieve toward 161, and the desk leans slightly against a fresh low; but the US-Japan rate gap is intact and disclosed intervention is still zero, so the tail stays open.

items.what: US employers added just 57,000 jobs in June with unemployment at 4.2%, and prior months were revised down by 74,000, cooling the case for a near-term Fed hike.
The Philadelphia semiconductor index fell 5.5% on 2 July, pulling the Nasdaq lower even as the Dow closed at a record after the soft jobs print.
Dutch TTF gas traded near €43.9 a megawatt-hour as Europe's heatwave kept power-and-gas demand elevated and gas the marginal fuel in the power stack.
